question_text: Explain why astronauts weigh less on the Moon than they do on Earth, even though their mass stays the same.
correct_answer: The Moon has a weaker gravitational field than Earth, so it exerts a smaller gravitational force on the astronauts. Weight depends on the gravitational field strength, not on mass.
explanation: Weight is the gravitational force acting on an object and depends on both the object's mass and the strength of the gravitational field. The Moon's weaker gravity means less force acts on the same mass, resulting in lower weight.
